
Scanned 8 Grok social signals and 18 arXiv papers. The arXiv papers are largely academic/theoretical with no direct Claude-specific practical tips. Among the Grok signals, three stand out as meeting ≥3 inclusion criteria with actionable, non-obvious Claude techniques. The XML tagging guide, Artifacts workflow, and computer use prompting tips are the strongest candidates not already in the README.

## Suggestions

- [Mastering Claude with XML Tags](https://overchatgpt.com/prompting/claude-xml-best-practices) — Practical guide to using XML tags for structured prompting in Claude — a non-obvious technique that significantly improves output reliability and parsing.
  Section: Tips of the Week | Criteria: Practical, actionable tip or technique for using Claude more effectively, Grounded in real usage evidence (community post, demo, benchmark, or documented workflow), Applicable to a broad audience (not highly domain-specific or niche), Novel or non-obvious — not a basic feature already in official docs

- [Best Practices for Claude Computer Use](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/computer-use#prompting-tips) — Official prompting tips for Claude's computer use beta — concrete guidance on structuring agentic tasks, handling failures, and scoping actions safely.
  Section: Tips of the Week | Criteria: Practical, actionable tip or technique for using Claude more effectively, Grounded in real usage evidence (community post, demo, benchmark, or documented workflow), Applicable to a broad audience (not highly domain-specific or niche)

- [Claude Artifacts Workflow Guide](https://aidavid.co/claude-artifacts-guide) — Step-by-step build/test loop workflows using Claude Artifacts — covers iterative prototyping patterns that go beyond basic artifact creation.
  Section: Tips of the Week | Criteria: Practical, actionable tip or technique for using Claude more effectively, Grounded in real usage evidence (community post, demo, benchmark, or documented workflow), Novel or non-obvious — not a basic feature already in official docs
